mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below 1% alongside gradual, telegraphed increases signals an accommodative policy stance that keeps the equity discount rate low, supporting persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during low-rate periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample mean (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE ticks up by 0.2 percentage points or more in a single month from a cyclical low, it signals an inflection in labor market tightness that triggers short-horizon risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.2pp month-over-month from a level below 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% near cycle lows as of 2017-05-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following triggered UNRATE upticks are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS accelerating) combined with a rising FEDFUNDS rate jointly signal an overheating economy prompting faster future tightening, which compresses valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; neither series alone carries this signal.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 2% AND FEDFUNDS up >= 0.25pp over prior 6 months
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce 60-day forward returns statistically indistinguishable from or greater than unconditional mean (p>0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 across two independent 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where retail sales (RSAFS) growth remains positive but is decelerating relative to trailing 6-month average, forward-looking equity markets price in slowing consumption momentum, producing muted or negative 20-day S&P 500 returns; in early/mid-cycle regimes the same deceleration is noise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth falls below its trailing 6-month average by more than 1pp
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (GDPC1 growth positive but decelerating)
failure_condition: The negative return effect fails to appear or reverses sign when conditioned on late-cycle GDPC1 growth deceleration, tested via subsample regression with p>0.10, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding real economic output and corporate earnings capacity, which supports higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.0998 with continued monthly gains as of 2017-05-15
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 6-month trend and forward 60d returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or negative INDPRO trends fail to coincide with negative return periods more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts (HOUST) below a critical threshold signals weakening forward-looking construction demand and household investment confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month as the market discounts weaker future GDP contribution.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1150 thousand units (month-over-month decline > 5%)
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1215.0 thousand units as of latest reading
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following triggered HOUST declines are positive in more than 55% of historical episodes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains low relative to inflation (implying deeply negative real rates), markets interpret this combination as a signal of imminent faster tightening, causing a short-horizon negative equity reaction within the following week; neither variable alone triggers this reaction.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.0% AND FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL YoY < -1.0pp (real rate below -1%)
regime_hint: early monetary tightening cycle with negative real rates
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during joint-trigger episodes are not significantly negative (p>0.10) relative to unconditional baseline, or effect disappears across two independent 2-year subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by unemployment near multi-year lows, a deceleration in payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) signals approaching labor market saturation and impending earnings growth slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; in early/mid-cycle regimes the same deceleration reflects healthy normalization and has no negative effect.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 6-month annualized growth decelerates by more than 0.5pp
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% near cycle lows as of 2017-05-15
failure_condition: The negative effect fails to materialize or reverses sign when conditioned on low-UNRATE late-cycle subsample (p>0.10 in regression interaction term), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
